costs: Data collection and reporting infrastructure costs
Opportunity cost of delayed action on a system with the EU's highest segregation levels [F-live-hungarian_education_system-03]
content.equity_impact: Neutral in the short term but risks entrenching Hungary's highest-in-EU segregation levels and the 53-point socioeconomic achievement gap, since no structural change addresses the mechanisms of stratification [F-live-portuguese_reform-03, F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-03].
expected_benefits: Avoids immediate political conflict with church maintainers and middle-class constituencies [F-live-political_feasibility-05]
Preserves existing infrastructure investment and stakeholder expectations
Generates evidence base for future, better-informed decisions
content.goal: Maintain current 6/8-year gimnázium structure while establishing systematic monitoring of segregation and equity outcomes to inform future decisions without immediate structural change.

implementation_steps.action: Mandate annual public reporting of school-level segregation indices, value-added by track, and socioeconomic composition using existing ABC testing infrastructure
Establish longitudinal panel tracking students across track types from Grade 5 through tertiary entry

mechanisms: Continued three-stage selection funnel at ages 10, 12, 14 preserves current stratification patterns [F-live-portuguese_reform-01]
Absence of legislative action reflects veto constituency dynamics (church-maintained schools, middle-class protection) [F-live-political_feasibility-05, F-live-political_feasibility-07]
No discrete reform proposal currently exists in tracking systems, consistent with continuation [F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-07]
risks: Segregation and inequality indicators continue worsening (170-point reading gap between advantaged/disadvantaged deciles) [F-live-political_feasibility-03]
Monitoring without action may be perceived as policy avoidance, reducing government credibility on stated equity concerns [F-live-legal_and_governance-06]
Demographic decline continues to concentrate advantaged students in tracks while comprehensive schools face closure pressure [F-live-demography-01, F-live-demography-07]
content.title: Status quo with monitoring (passive counterfactual)
